sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All 3 clicks were `no_impact` - the bottom blue button already hit its right limit. The green object can't reach the gap by shifting right alone. I've been wasting actions. Let me reconsider the puzzle completely.
I need to understand what the middle blue button does. Maybe it controls the black bar section (rows 40-43) which contains the gap. If I can shift the gap LEFT to meet the green object, that could work.
Let me check the current state and try the middle blue button.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if node['color'] in ['N', 'c'] and min(rows) > 0:
        print(f"  color={node['color']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
    if node['color'] == 'B':
        print(f"  B rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
    if node['color'] == 'W' and min(rows) > 0:
        print(f"  W rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
